summary: Completed full frontend-only end-to-end testing of PolarisOrigin marketing site (Home, Services, Case Studies, About, Careers, Contact, 404, Navbar, Footer) across desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x844). All 51 assertions passed; 0 failures.
critical_code_review_comments: Code is well structured with consistent use of data-testid attributes on all interactive elements.
Contact form submission is intentionally a client-side mocked flow with 900ms delay (no network call) — confirmed by app code and matches review_request expectations.
No bg-gradient utility classes anywhere on rendered DOM; brand color #1f3a5f and gold accents used consistently with sharp-edged (rounded-none) buttons and lucide-react line icons only.
Case Studies filter uses sticky header at top-[72px] which correctly aligns with navbar height.
Sonner Toaster is configured at root in App.js with brand-consistent styling.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified flows: (1) Sticky navbar with POLARIS·ORIGIN lockup and all 4 nav links + Book Free Consultation CTA at desktop; mobile hamburger toggles nav-mobile-panel with CTA; (2) Home hero contains required keywords ('Hyper-personalized', 'books meetings', 'show-ups'); hero-cta-primary -> /contact, hero-cta-secondary -> /case-studies; (3) Services page has service-pillar-01..04 + services-cta -> /contact; (4) Case Studies renders 6 cards by default, filter-logistics-saas shows 2, filter-all restores 6, cases-cta -> /contact; (5) About has about-cta -> /contact; (6) Careers roles-accordion has 4 roles, expand reveals apply link to /contact; (7) Contact form: all 8 field-* testids present; empty submit shows 'A few fields are still empty' toast and does NOT navigate; invalid email shows valid-email toast; happy path with shadcn Selects (using force-click on options) completes within ~2s, shows contact-success with 'Adelaide' greeting; contact-submit-another restores empty form; (8) Footer with footer-cta to /contact rendered on all pages; (9) /__nope__ renders 404 with 'Return Home' link back to /. No bg-gradient classes. No backend; nothing to retest.
